title: Study Abroad Program Inquiry
transcript: Student: Professor Davis, do you have a moment? I'm interested in the study abroad program in Japan for next spring, and I heard you're the faculty coordinator. Professor: I am. Great to hear you're interested. Have you looked at the program website? Student: Yes, but I have a few questions. First, do I need to have completed any specific language requirements? Professor: No prior Japanese is required. The program includes an intensive language course during the first month. However, you do need to have completed at least two semesters of college-level coursework. Student: I've completed three semesters, so that's fine. What about housing? Professor: You'll stay with a host family for the first semester. That's meant to help with cultural immersion and language practice. After that, you can move into a shared apartment if you prefer. Student: That sounds great. How about credits? Will my courses transfer back? Professor: All courses are pre-approved for transfer credit. You just need to get your specific course selections signed off by your academic advisor before you go. Student: Is there any financial aid available for the program? Professor: Yes, there are scholarships specifically for study abroad students. The application deadline for those is November 1st, so you still have time. Student: Wonderful. I'll start working on the application this week.

questions.stem: Why does the student speak with Professor Davis?
options: To get a recommendation letter for a scholarship
To ask about the study abroad program in Japan
To change his major to Japanese studies
To complain about a housing assignment
questions.explanation: The student is interested in the study abroad program in Japan and has several questions about requirements, housing, credits, and financial aid.

questions.stem: What does the professor say about housing in the program?
options: Housing arrangements are the student's responsibility
Students stay with a host family first, then may move to a shared apartment
All students share apartments near campus
questions.explanation: The professor explains that students stay with a host family for the first semester and can move to a shared apartment afterward.

audioSegments.text: Professor Davis, do you have a moment? I'm interested in the study abroad program in Japan for next spring, and I heard you're the faculty coordinator.
I am. Great to hear you're interested. Have you looked at the program website?
Yes, but I have a few questions. First, do I need to have completed any specific language requirements?
No prior Japanese is required. The program includes an intensive language course during the first month. However, you do need to have completed at least two semesters of college-level coursework.
I've completed three semesters, so that's fine. What about housing?
You'll stay with a host family for the first semester. That's meant to help with cultural immersion and language practice. After that, you can move into a shared apartment if you prefer.
That sounds great. How about credits? Will my courses transfer back?
All courses are pre-approved for transfer credit. You just need to get your specific course selections signed off by your academic advisor before you go.
Is there any financial aid available for the program?
Yes, there are scholarships specifically for study abroad students. The application deadline for those is November 1st, so you still have time.
Wonderful. I'll start working on the application this week.
